body.title: Remove legacy .env — config lives in cfg files
body.description:
config.py was loading .env before the cfg files on every startup, even though the migration to igor.switches.cfg / igor.credentials.cfg / igor.cfg completed long ago (migration_001). This meant any accidental .env write would silently shadow cfg values. Fixed: removed .env load from config.py, deleted the stale .env (which had IGOR_INFERENCE_OVERRIDE written to it last session but was loading before cfg files so got overridden anyway), moved IGOR_INFERENCE_OVERRIDE=akiendelllinux to igor.switches.cfg where it belongs.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/config.py, ~/.TheIgors/Igor-wild-0001/igor.switches.cfg
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** remove .env load path from config.py; add IGOR_INFERENCE_OVERRIDE to switches.cfg; delete .env. No changes to env_sync.py (.env fallback there is intentional for pre-migration instances).
**Test plan:** config.py unit tests pass; igor.switches.cfg load order verified via config.dump()
